heading: New energy efficient home credit
label: 45L New energy efficient home credit

children.label: A
children.text: constructed by the eligible contractor, and

children.label: B
children.text: acquired by a person from such eligible contractor for use as a residence during the taxable year.

children.label: i
children.text: which meets the requirements of subsection (c)(1)(A) (and which does not meet the requirements of subsection (c)(1)(B)), $2,500, and

children.label: ii
children.text: which meets the requirements of subsection (c)(1)(B), $5,000, and

children.label: i
children.text: which meets the requirements of subsection (c)(1)(A) (and which does not meet the requirements of subsection (c)(1)(B)), $500, and

children.label: ii
children.text: which meets the requirements of subsection (c)(1)(B), $1,000.

children.label: A
children.text: the person who constructed the qualified new energy efficient home, or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a qualified new energy efficient home which is a manufactured home, the manufactured home producer of such home.

children.label: A
children.text: located in the United States,

children.label: B
children.text: the construction of which is substantially completed after the date of the enactment of this section, and

children.label: C
children.text: which meets the energy saving requirements of subsection (c).

children.heading: Construction
children.label: 3 Construction
children.text: The term “construction” includes substantial reconstruction and rehabilitation.

children.heading: Acquire
children.label: 4 Acquire
children.text: The term “acquire” includes purchase.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A dwelling unit meets the requirements of this subparagraph if such dwelling unit meets the requirements of paragraph (2) or (3) (whichever is applicable).

children.heading: Zero energy ready home program
children.label: B Zero energy ready home program
children.text: A dwelling unit meets the requirements of this subparagraph if such dwelling unit is certified as a zero energy ready home under the zero energy ready home program of the Department of Energy as in effect on (or any successor program determined by the Secretary).January 1, 2023

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of a dwelling unit acquired before , the Energy Star Single-Family New Homes National Program Requirements 3.1, orJanuary 1, 2025

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a dwelling unit acquired after , the Energy Star Single-Family New Homes National Program Requirements 3.2, andDecember 31, 2024

children.label: ii
children.text: the most recent Energy Star Single-Family New Homes Program Requirements applicable to the location of such dwelling unit (as in effect on the latter of , or January 1 of two calendar years prior to the date the dwelling unit was acquired), orJanuary 1, 2023

children.label: B
children.text: such dwelling unit meets the most recent Energy Star Manufactured Home National program requirements as in effect on the latter of , or January 1 of two calendar years prior to the date such dwelling unit is acquired.January 1, 2023

children.label: A
children.text: such dwelling unit meets the most recent Energy Star Multifamily New Construction National Program Requirements (as in effect on either , or January 1 of three calendar years prior to the date the dwelling was acquired, whichever is later), andJanuary 1, 2023

children.label: B
children.text: such dwelling unit meets the most recent Energy Star Multifamily New Construction Regional Program Requirements applicable to the location of such dwelling unit (as in effect on either , or January 1 of three calendar years prior to the date the dwelling was acquired, whichever is later).January 1, 2023

children.heading: Method of certification
children.label: 1 Method of certification
children.text: A certification described in subsection (c) shall be made in accordance with guidance prescribed by the Secretary, after consultation with the Secretary of Energy. Such guidance shall specify procedures and methods for calculating energy and cost savings.

children.heading: Form
children.label: 2 Form
children.text: Any certification described in subsection (c) shall be made in writing in a manner which specifies in readily verifiable fashion the energy efficient building envelope components and energy efficient heating or cooling equipment installed and their respective rated energy efficiency performance.

children.heading: Basis adjustment
children.label: e Basis adjustment
children.text: For purposes of this subtitle, if a credit is allowed under this section in connection with any expenditure for any property, the increase in the basis of such property which would (but for this subsection) result from such expenditure shall be reduced by the amount of the credit so determined. This subsection shall not apply for purposes of determining the adjusted basis of any building under section 42.

children.heading: Coordination with investment credit
children.label: f Coordination with investment credit
children.text: For purposes of this section, expenditures taken into account under section 47 or 48(a) shall not be taken into account under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: $2,500 in the case of a residence which meets the requirements of subparagraph (A) of subsection (c)(1) (and which does not meet the requirements of subparagraph (B) of such subsection), and

children.label: B
children.text: $5,000 in the case of a residence which meets the requirements of subsection (c)(1)(B).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The requirements described in this subparagraph with respect to any qualified residence are that the taxpayer shall ensure that any laborers and mechanics employed by the taxpayer or any contractor or subcontractor in the construction of such residence shall be paid wages at rates not less than the prevailing rates for construction, alteration, or repair of a similar character in the locality in which such residence is located as most recently determined by the Secretary of Labor, in accordance with subchapter IV of chapter 31 of title 40, United States Code.

children.heading: Correction and penalty related to failure to satisfy wage requirements
children.label: B Correction and penalty related to failure to satisfy wage requirements
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of section 45(b)(7)(B) shall apply.

children.heading: Regulations and guidance
children.label: 3 Regulations and guidance
children.text: The Secretary shall issue such regulations or other guidance as the Secretary determines necessary to carry out the purposes of this subsection, including regulations or other guidance which provides for requirements for recordkeeping or information reporting for purposes of administering the requirements of this subsection.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: h Termination
children.text: This section shall not apply to any qualified new energy efficient home acquired after .June 30, 2026
